title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 21st July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.price: buy on dips; caller bought at 325
recommendations.note: His most favourite stock and birthday-gift pick for viewers - still buyable even after the run-up.
recommendations.detail: Asked to gift the viewers a stock for the anchor's birthday, Kutumba Rao said top of mind for him is only one - Capston (as heard), his most favourite stock, which he has been repeating for four years. It is a small company but has got very good growth potential, and even now, after it has risen this much, you can still buy it; he knows the many good qualities in it. Buy it on dips. Caller Sravani, who bought about 2,000 shares at 325 on his earlier suggestion and has money for another 2,000, was told to keep buying when dips come - she is in a good stock, and to hold what she already has.

recommendations.note: Large-caps are still under-priced; hold until you become a senior citizen.
recommendations.detail: Asked at the end of the show for his final stock idea, Kutumba Rao said valuations in mid-caps and small-caps feel pricey while large-caps are still under-priced, and if asked, he would buy L&T. He added that he is telling investors to hold L&T until they become a senior citizen, because over another 10 years tremendous wealth will be created in it.

recommendations.note: Buy on every decline; Bhogapuram airport opening on August 1 is an added feather.
recommendations.detail: GMR Airports appeared on his delivery-conviction list. He recalled that when it corrected to 90 he said the stock crosses 100 easily and could reach 130 to 140 in the medium to long term; it has now come up to 112, and on every decline it is still a bet. He added that the Bhogapuram airport near Visakhapatnam starts on August 1 - one of the beautiful airports, which he has already seen and which is coming out extremely well - and that it will be an added feather for GMR Airports.

recommendations.price: bought at 290, now ~350; trading stop 320-330; can reach 550-600 in a year
recommendations.note: Recommended from 160; a possible top performer and the country's second largest port operator.
recommendations.detail: Caller Madhuvarni from Gudivada bought JSW Infra at 290 and is in profit at around 350. Kutumba Rao said she bought at a good price and to hold - he has been recommending the stock from 160 as one that could be among the top performers, because after Adani Ports the maximum ports come under JSW, and JSW moves aggressively as it expands in that field. The stock is near a 52-week high; hold it continuously with a trading stop-loss around 320-330. The stock can go up to 550-600 levels and he would not be surprised even if it reaches that within the next one year. JSW Infra is the second largest port operator in the country.

recommendations.note: Should not be sold at this time; largest lender to state governments with stable margins.
recommendations.detail: Gousia Syed (Gousia Begum) mailed asking whether to sell HUDCO at Rs 45 and shift into another sector. Kutumba Rao said HUDCO should not be sold at this time, because with the changes coming in financing HUDCO is becoming the largest lender to state governments for housing projects and the like - including over Rs 10,000 crore in Amaravati - and most recently it has made a fresh tie-up with Odisha and is funding there. Stable margins will continue irrespective of global changes or RBI regulation changes. He would hold on to HUDCO, one of the stronger public sector finance companies. (Note: on 17 July he had said exit HUDCO on rallies; this answer, at a Rs 45 price, is an explicit Hold.)

recommendations.note: Average it - result already discounted; biologics move gives a first-mover advantage.
recommendations.detail: Siddharth Sai holds 500 shares of Vimta Labs at 650 and asked whether to average. Kutumba Rao said he would say average it: their result has come and the market has already discounted it, and the stock is stable around 540. The numbers are year-on-year better but quarter-on-quarter flattish. But the company is moving into new markets with a first-mover advantage - as it had earlier in fast-moving electrical goods, and now with the move into biologics - and in testing services the company can do extremely well from here.

recommendations.note: Entered at a high; a decent company in a long consumption-theme consolidation.
recommendations.detail: Parameswar mailed that he is at a 14% loss on VBL bought at 545. Kutumba Rao said he entered at a high in a stock that had risen a lot, and on a long-term basis the stock is now in a long consolidation. This is the consumption theme, where stocks sometimes sit in long consolidations because results from the new geographies they entered are still to come. For now it is a decent company and he would say hold - it is not a stock that rises all the time, since Varun Beverages is based on the consumption theme. The anchor added their standing rule that VBL is a summer stock - buy at the start of summer and sell as summer ends - and Kutumba Rao agreed that AC stocks and cool-drink stocks behave the same way, bought in winter and sold in summer.

recommendations.note: Don't book the loss; hold until it comes to profitability, don't sell at this price.
recommendations.detail: Manoj Goud is down 60% on Network 18 and asked whether to average or book the loss. Kutumba Rao said do not book the loss, because consolidation in the media space is finishing. The only thing is that, knowing it sits under a big industrial group, many people bought it, and at one stage he too called it a dark horse. It has large turnovers but is not coming to profitability; until it comes to profitability he would say hold, and as of now it should not be sold at this price.

recommendations.note: A very weak counter, as he has said many times; yesterday's 20% pop was news-driven.
recommendations.detail: Discussing the new action starting in speculative counters, Kutumba Rao said many people used to ask about Som Distilleries and that he has said many times it is a very weak counter. Yesterday, all at once - call it results or news-driven activity - it touched 20%. He used it as an example of why one should take a stock-specific and sector-specific view and not chase stocks, since the market looks slightly fragile and caution is very much necessary.
